quoteFirst: To see Sevillian society out of doors, go to the Parque Maria Luisa and the adjoining Paseo de las Delicias
quoteLast: . This is the fashionable promenade, and here the _élite_ of the city drive in open carriages daily. The costumes of the _señoras_ are varied and stylish.

author: Gallichan, Walter M. (Walter Matthew), 1861-1946; Hartley, C. Gasquoine (Catherine Gasquoine), 1867-1928 [Contributor]

quoteLast: , was now, by the way-bill of the regular Post-coach, irrevocably fixed for my departure. I had still half a day to order my house; from which, for two nights and two days and a half, my breast, its breastwork and palisado, was now, along with my Self, to be withdrawn.
title: The Campaner Thal, and Other Writings

quoteFirst: We passed through several villages and small towns surrounded by snow-covered fields and frozen ponds. Nothing of note happened to vary the monotony of the continual shaking of our motors.
quoteLast: we came to a standstill, and looking out, found ourselves in what seemed to be a good-sized town. We were not left long in suspense, for soon the cheery face of the officer in charge appeared, enquiring where we wished to be driven, for we were at St. Omer.
title: The Irish Nuns at Ypres: An Episode of the War
author: Columban, M., Dame; Redmond, John Edward, 1856-1918 [Author of introduction, etc.]; O'Brien, R. Barry (Richard Barry), 1847-1918 [Editor]
